evals.prompt: Getting this error on the call reviews upload route: 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'text')'. It's happening when the Whisper transcription comes back — seems like the response shape changed or something. Here's the error from the logs: 'at processTranscription (src/app/api/call-reviews/upload/route.ts:87:34)'
evals.expected_output: Classifies as EXTERNAL (OpenAI Whisper API response shape), fires the Research Gate via Perplexity before attempting any fix, reads the actual route file, and only proposes a fix after confirming the current Whisper API response shape.
expectations: Announces the /debug pipeline at the start of the response
Reads the actual file src/app/api/call-reviews/upload/route.ts before proposing a fix
Classifies the bug as EXTERNAL before attempting any code change
Uses Perplexity or similar research tool to check current OpenAI Whisper API response shape before fixing
Does NOT propose a code fix before completing the research step

evals.prompt: The call review list is showing 'Unknown' for prospect_name on every row, even for calls where I can see the name in the database. This just started happening — I didn't change anything obvious. Can you figure out what's broken?
evals.expected_output: Classifies as INTERNAL (data fetch/mapping bug within src/), goes straight to code investigation (no research gate), traces the data flow from the DB fetch to the UI component, and identifies the specific disconnect.
expectations: Classifies the bug as INTERNAL (not EXTERNAL, not ENVIRONMENT)
Does NOT fire a Perplexity/research search for this internally-scoped bug
Reads relevant call review component or data-fetching code to trace the issue
Checks recent git changes on relevant files to see if something was recently modified
States the root cause before proposing a fix

evals.prompt: Google Ads OAuth works perfectly on localhost but in production on Vercel I'm getting a 500 error right after the user clicks 'Connect to Google Ads'. The callback URL never gets hit. Works 100% of the time locally, fails 100% of the time in prod.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as ENVIRONMENT, checks for missing env vars in Vercel, checks HMAC state secret, checks runtime declarations, and checks for Edge vs Node.js runtime issues before touching code.
expectations: Classifies the bug as ENVIRONMENT (works locally, fails in prod)
Recommends checking Vercel env vars (does not print values, just confirms their existence)
Checks for export const runtime = 'nodejs' declaration on OAuth-related routes since they use crypto
Does not attempt to fix code before confirming env vars and runtime config are correct
References the relevant Google Ads OAuth files (src/app/api/google-ads/ or src/lib/google-ads/)
